When someone says "I'm non-binary" what I hear is "There's a certain way men & women should look & act, and I don't want to go along w that"
There's nothing wrong w defying traditional gender roles I guess, but to say "I'm not a woman bc women act & look like X" is pretty sexist
A generation ago you would have been called tomboy or butch, now you're just rejecting the label of woman bc you associate it w gender roles
When I identified as non-binary it was because I felt there was a certain way women had to act and I didn't felt like that fit me
Instead of aiming to destroy gender, the truly revolutionary move would be to show people that it's ok to be a butch woman or effeminate man
Btw this has absolutely nothing to do with actual transsexuals who suffer from gender dysphoria & need to transition for their wellbeing
This is about the growing movement of young ppl who reject their gender simply bc they don't like it, ppl who confuse gender w/ personality
